zh.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish於1319年之前與Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish于1319年之前与Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Potton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Potton (Beds), which is recorded as Potone in 1086 and Potton in 1203. The place-name appears to derive from Old English pot(t) ‘pot’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Cavendish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Suffolk named Cavendish, from an Old English byname Cāfna (meaning ‘bold, daring’) + Old English edisc ‘enclosed pasture’. Cavendish is the surname of the Dukes of Devonshire. They are descended from Sir John Cavendish, who served as Chief Justice of the King's Bench (1372–81). Under the Tudors and Stuarts, a succession of shrewd and powerful heads of the family achieved political prominence and became extremely wealthy. William Cavendish, 2nd Earl of Devonshire, was a strong supporter of the ‘Glorious Revolution’ of 1688 and of William of Orange (William III) against James II. In 1694 he was created Duke of Devonshire. The Latin family motto, Cavendo tutus ‘safe through taking care’ is a pun on the surname.

Manx: Anglicized form of Corjeag, a shortened form of Mac Quartag ‘son of Svarteygr’ (from Old Norse, meaning ‘black-eyed’). Folk etymology interpreted Corjeag incorrectly as Manx curjeig ‘alms-dish’ and equally incorrectly supposed that the surname Cavendish was a synonym meaning ‘giving-dish’. Manxmen's familiarity with the English surname presumably led to the substitution.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish married Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish before 1319, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish a épousé Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish avant 1319, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish hat vor 1319 in Europa Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish lui sposato Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish prima del 1319, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendishは1319年以前にヨーロッパでMrs. John Potton of Cavendishと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish(은)는 1319년 이전 우럽에서 Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish casou-se com Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish antes 1319, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish женился на женщине по имени Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish перед 1319 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: John Potton of Cavendish se casó con Mrs. John Potton of Cavendish antes de 1319, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
